title: 10 best herbal essential oils for acne
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for acne are natural plant extracts that help to reduce inflammation, fight bacteria, and soothe skin irritations, making them an effective alternative to traditional acne treatments. The best herbal essential oils for acne include Melaleuca alternifolia, Lavandula angustifolia, Rosmarinus officinalis, Cymbopogon citratus, Aloe vera, Aloe barbadensis, Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, Zingiber officinale, and Calendula officinalis. If left untreated, acne can lead to emotional distress, low self-esteem, and even impact daily life, causing people to avoid social situations and feel isolated due to the physical and emotional scarring it can cause.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree essential oils helps with acne because of its powerful antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria that cause breakouts. When applied topically to the skin, tea tree oil penetrates deep into pores, killing Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that can clog pores and lead to inflammation. This natural antibacterial agent also reduces redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion. Additionally, its antifungal properties help control the growth of yeast on the skin, further contributing to its acne-fighting benefits.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Tea Tree Essential Oil has antibacterial properties that can help to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which is a common cause of acne.
Anti-inflammatory property: Tea Tree Essential Oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne.
Antifungal property: Tea Tree Essential Oil has antifungal properties that can help to combat fungal infections that can cause acne-like symptoms, such as fungal acne or pityrosporum folliculitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are rich in cineole, a compound that has potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating acne-prone skin.
Stems: The stems contain a high concentration of terpinen-4-ol, an important compound with antimicrobial activity that helps combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melaleuca alternifolia leaves at dawn when essential oil content is highest using pruning shears for precise cutting.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloths for 7-10 days, monitoring humidity levels below 60%.
Steam distill 500g of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves with 1l of high-quality water at 100°c for 4-6 hours to extract essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove impurities, allowing clear liquid to pass while retaining particles.
Bottle and store the pure melaleuca alternifolia essential oil in airtight glass containers away from direct sunlight and heat sources for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender essential oils helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Lavender oil has been shown to reduce acne-causing bacteria on the skin, minimizing breakouts and preventing future occurrences. Its antiseptic qualities also help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with pimples. Additionally, lavender's astringent properties can help to shrink pores, further diminishing the appearance of acne scars and blemishes.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: English Lavender essential oil's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which is a common cause of acne.

Anti-inflammatory properties: English Lavender essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it less noticeable and more manageable.

Antioxidant properties: English Lavender essential oil's antioxidant properties can help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating acne caused by bacterial infections.
Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin, making them useful in soothing acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested material by spreading it thin on paper towels for 24 hours followed by oven drying at 35°c for 3 days.
Steam distill 100g of dried lavender material using 500ml of water for 2 hours to obtain approximately 15ml of essential oil.
Filter the resulting essential oil through a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight lids.
Add 10 drops of the lavandula angustifolia essential oil to a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil for use in acne treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe essential oils helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It reduces redness and swelling by soothing irritated skin, while also combating bacteria that can cause breakouts. The oil's natural astringent properties help to tighten pores, reducing the appearance of pimples and preventing future occurrences. Additionally, aloe vera's ability to regulate sebum production helps to balance the skin's natural moisture levels, reducing excess oil that can clog pores and exacerbate acne.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective ingredient in soothing and calming the skin.

Antibacterial properties: Aloe vera's antibacterial agents, including anthraquinones and phenolic acids, combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin.

Wound-healing properties: Aloe vera's wound-healing compounds, such as vitamin E and beta-carotene, accelerate the repair process by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue elasticity, and enhancing the growth of new skin cells.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside aloe leaves contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help reduce inflammation and soothe acne-prone skin.
Roots: Aloe roots have antibacterial properties that can help combat acne-causing bacteria, promoting clear and healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from your garden or store-bought aloe vera gel, containing 90-95% water.
Chop the aloe vera leaves into small pieces and soak them in cold water for 4 hours to extract the juice.
Combine the extracted aloe vera juice with 10% of dried calendula flowers weighing 50 grams per 500 milliliters.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 days under indirect sunlight, shaking the container twice daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, preserving the filtered liquid as your herbal aloe vera essential oil.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary essential oils helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The oil's antibacterial properties also combat P. acnes bacteria that cause acne, preventing further inflammation and infection. Additionally, rosemary oil's antifungal properties help regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil and clogged pores. By balancing the skin's natural pH and combating acne-causing bacteria, rosemary essential oils provide a natural solution for tackling acne and promoting healthy, clear skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Rosemary essential oil has antibacterial properties that help kill P. acnes bacteria, a common cause of acne, thereby reducing inflammation and preventing new breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory property: Rosemary essential oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, helping to calm the skin and promote healing.

Antioxidant property: Rosemary essential oil's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from environmental stressors, such as pollution and UV damage, which can contribute to acne development by reducing oxidative stress and promoting collagen production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, rosemary roots help to reduce inflammation and prevent scarring caused by acne.
Buds: Rosemary buds contain high levels of camphor, which has antibacterial properties that combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that cause acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh rosemary leaves at dawn when dew is still present for optimal potency.
Steam distill the rosemary leaves in a clean glass apparatus for 30 minutes with 100ml of distilled water.
Collect the resulting essential oil and separate it from the hydrosol by decantation within 1 hour.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel to remove any impurities after 10 minutes.
Transfer the filtered essential oil to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with acne because of its antibacterial properties, which allow it to combat the underlying causes of acne. The oil's ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) reduces inflammation and prevents new breakouts. Additionally, thyme oil's antifungal properties help to regulate the skin's natural pH balance, reducing excess oil production and unclogging pores. This results in clearer, healthier-looking skin that is less prone to acne.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Thyme essential oil has antibacterial properties that help kill P.acnes bacteria, which is a common cause of acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant compounds in thyme essential oil help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development, promoting healthy skin cell turnover and reducing oxidative stress.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and inflammation associated with acne, making it an effective natural treatment for soothing and calming acne-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with acne.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant properties that can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a sustainable source in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested leaves using a clean cloth or paper towels for at least four hours.
Steam distill the dried leaves with water in a still using a 1:10 ratio of herb to water for two hours.
Filter the resulting essential oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and store it at room temperature.
Allow the essential oil to mature for six weeks before use, shaking the bottle daily to ensure even blending.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum essential oils helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains eucalyptol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial overgrowth that can contribute to acne. Additionally, Tasmanian blue gum's natural astringent properties help to dry out pimples and reduce their appearance. By incorporating this essential oil into one's skincare routine, individuals can effectively treat and prevent breakouts, leaving their skin looking clearer and more radiant.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil exhibits antibacterial activity, which can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne development.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum essential oil is rich in antioxidants, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and lead to premature aging, scarring, or post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (PIH) caused by acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them effective in treating acne by reducing inflammation and combating bacteria.

Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian blue gum plant contain terpenes and flavonoids that exhibit antimicrobial activity, helping to prevent the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a well-known organic source for maximum potency.
Clean and wash the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves thoroughly in cold running water.
Steam distill the cleaned eucalyptus globulus leaves at 100°c for 4 hours to release essential oil.
Filter the resulting steam-distilled essential oil mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove impurities.
Bottle and store the pure eucalyptus globulus essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium essential oils helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's germicidal action targets and eliminates Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to clogged pores and breakouts. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while its antioxidant compounds work to combat oxidative stress and environmental factors that can exacerbate acne. As a result, wild geranium essential oils can help regulate skin health and promote clear, balanced complexions.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Rose Geranium essential oil has antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that causes acne.
Anti-inflammatory property: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, promoting a faster healing process.
Hormone-balancing property: Rose Geranium essential oil has been traditionally used to balance hormones in women, which can contribute to acne caused by hormonal imbalances.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antibacterial compounds, which help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
Rhyzomes: High in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest geranium maculatum leaves at dawn when they are most potent and fragrant containing 10-20% geraniol.
Wash the harvested leaves thoroughly with cold distilled water to remove dirt and debris.
Steam distill the washed leaves in a stainless steel apparatus for 4 hours using 5 liters of water at 100°c.
Collect the resulting essential oil that contains 15-30% geraniol and has antibacterial properties.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities or sedimentation occurring after storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass essential oils helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacteria that cause breakouts. The oil's high levels of citral and geraniol also help to reduce inflammation and soothe redness, promoting a clearer complexion. Additionally, lemongrass has natural antifungal and antioxidant properties that work to prevent clogged pores and protect the skin from environmental stressors, making it an effective addition to any acne treatment routine.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass essential oil has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a common cause of acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of cymbopogon citratus and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steam distill the harvested leaves for approximately 3-4 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the obtained essential oil in a clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place.
Add a few drops of lavender oil to enhance the calming properties of the cymbopogon citratus essential oil.
Mix the prepared essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or jojoba oil for a 3% dilution ratio.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold essential oils helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's active compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also targeting the bacteria that cause breakouts. Additionally, pot marigold essential oil's antiseptic properties help to prevent further infection and promote wound healing, making it an effective natural remedy for clearing up acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: This property helps to combat bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), by inhibiting their growth and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula oil's anti-inflammatory effects reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, allowing for faster healing and reduced scarring.

Antioxidant property: Calendula oil's antioxidant activity helps to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress, which can contribute to acne development by damaging skin cells and leading to inflammation.

Flowers: The fragrant flowers of the pot marigold plant contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin tone.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried calendula flowers at dawn for optimal potency and therapeutic properties.
Dry the harvested calendula flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 60°c for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried calendula flowers with 20% water in a copper or stainless steel still for 4-6 hours to extract the essential oil.
Filter the distilled essential oil through a coffee filter or paper towel into dark glass bottles, discarding any impurities or sediment.
Store the obtained calendula essential oil in an airtight container at room temperature, away from direct sunlight and heat sources, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet annie essential oils helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that cause breakouts. The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds also reduce redness and swelling associated with acne-prone skin. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Sweet Annie Essential Oil has antimicrobial properties that help to combat Pseudomonas aeruginosa, a common bacteria that causes acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory property: The oil's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, promoting healing and calming the skin.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne inflammation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia annua leaves and flowers in the morning.
Dry the plant material using air circulation or desiccant methods for 7-10 days.
Steam distill the dried plant material using a glass still or copper still at atmospheric pressure.
Collect the essential oil in a clean, sterilized container within 30 minutes of distillation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter or cheesecloth to remove any impurities.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat acne with herbal essential oils is by combining lavender oil's antibacterial properties with tea tree oil's antiseptic qualities. Mix a few drops of each oil with a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil and apply topically to affected areas twice daily. Additionally, chamomile oil can help soothe inflammation, while geranium oil can balance hormonal fluctuations that contribute to acne. Regular application can reduce acne severity and prevent scarring.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal essential oils are various skin issues such as eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of herbal essential oils like tea tree oil, lavender oil, and geranium oil help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation. These oils can also help to balance the skin's pH levels, promoting a healthy and clear complexion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which have been shown to reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes. It also has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, Melaleuca alternifolia oil can help to unclog pores and promote healthy skin, making it a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: A monoterpenoid that exhibits antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin.
Linalool: A terpene with anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Cymene: A terpene with antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, helping to prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are primarily used to make essential oils for acne due to their high content of compounds like cineole, which exhibit antimicrobial properties.

Stems: The stems of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils for acne because they contain a significant amount of compounds like terpinen-4-ol, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

Flowers: The flowers of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make essential oils for acne due to their high concentration of compounds like geraniol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature fresh leaves of the plant, using sterile scissors to prevent contamination, in the morning after dew has fallen.
Steam distill the leaves, using a distillation apparatus, for approximately 3-4 hours to release the oils from the plant material.
Collect the distilled oil, separating it from the water using a separator or decanter, resulting in a yellowish liquid.
Filter the oil, using a paper or cloth filter, to remove any remaining plant debris or sediment, taking about 30 minutes.
Bottle the filtered oil, storing it in a dark glass container, away from heat and light, to preserve its quality and potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause breakouts can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to acne. By promoting skin health and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help to regulate the skin's natural balance and reduce the occurrence of acne. remedy_constituents: Linalool: A terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).
Linalyl acetate: A terpene that has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce acne symptoms and prevent the formation of new acne lesions.
Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene with potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils for acne because they contain a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce acne.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for acne because they contain a smaller amount of linalool and linalyl acetate, but also other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause acne.

Stems: The stems are used to make essential oils for acne because they contain a mixture of compounds, including linalool and linalyl acetate, that help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that cause acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature lavandula angustifolia flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated to prevent contamination.
Steam distill 50 grams of the harvested flowers in 200 milliliters of water at 100 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled vapor and condense it into a liquid using a condenser coil in 2 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a separating funnel or a centrifuge in 30 minutes.
Store the extracted lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils helps with acne because of its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's high concentration of carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid helps to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can clog pores and lead to acne. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties help to protect the skin from environmental stressors and promote healthy skin cell growth, reducing the appearance of acne and preventing future breakouts. remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene helps with acne by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Bornyl acetate: This monoterpene has antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the growth of bacteria (such as Propionibacterium acnes) that contribute to acne development.
Rosmarinus officinalis acid (ROA): This phenolic compound has antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to the reduction of acne symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, which help control acne-causing bacteria.
Flowers: Known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Stems: Contain antibacterial compounds that help prevent the spread of acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves in the early morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Steam distill the leaves at 100-150°c for 2-3 hours using a ratio of 1:1 leaf to water.
Collect the distilled essential oil and separate it from the water using a separation funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and ensure clarity.
Store the rosmarinus officinalis essential oil in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and keep refrigerated at 4-8°c.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps with acne because it contains natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Geranial: Exhibits potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for making essential oils as they contain high levels of citral, a compound known for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to produce essential oils rich in limonene, which helps to reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of acne-causing bacteria.
Stems: Stems are used to produce essential oils with a high concentration of geraniol, a compound that has been shown to have antibacterial properties and help to reduce acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the mature stalks of cymbopogon citratus by cutting them at the base of the plant using sharp scissors.
Steam distill the harvested stalks in a still to extract the essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the condenser tube and transfer it to a glass container.
Filter the essential oil using a paper filter to remove impurities and particles.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed tightly to preserve its quality.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 mature leaves from the aloe barbadensis plant on a sunny day for optimal potency.

Remove the outer skin of the leaves using a sharp knife or blade to reveal the gel inside.
Extract the gel from the leaves using a centrifuge or a juicer with a fine mesh filter.
Steam distill the extracted gel for 2-3 hours to produce the essential oil using a distillation apparatus.

remedy_constituents: Cineole: This monoterpene helps reduce acne by its antibacterial properties, which inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes bacteria that cause acne.
Alpha-pinene: This monoterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which help reduce redness and prevent infection associated with acne.
Limonene: This diterpene exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative stress that can contribute to acne development.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part for making eucalyptus essential oil, which is known for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce acne.
Barks: The bark of Eucalyptus globulus is also used to make essential oil, which is believed to have antioxidant properties that can help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 kg of eucalyptus globulus leaves from mature plants during early morning to ensure optimal oil content.
Steam distill the eucalyptus globulus leaves at 100°c for 3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separator or decanter within 1 hour.
Filter the essential oil through a paper filter to remove impurities and ensure clarity within 30 minutes.
Bottle the filtered essential oil in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids to preserve its aroma and shelf life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils helps with acne because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that contributes to acne. Additionally, the oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. As a result, Thymus vulgaris essential oils can help to clear pores and promote healthy skin, making it an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Carvacrol: Exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation associated with breakouts.
Thymol: Demonstrates strong antibacterial activity, targeting Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne development.
Caryophyllene: Displays anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that exacerbate acne symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are the primary source of thymol, a key component in essential oils that helps to reduce inflammation and combat acne-causing bacteria.
Flowers: Thyme flowers are used to produce essential oils rich in geraniol and linalool, which have antimicrobial properties that help to control acne and promote skin health.
Buds: Thyme buds are another source of essential oils containing thymol and other compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the early morning when the plant is at its highest oil concentration.
Steam distill the harvested leaves with 10 parts plant material to 1 part water for 2 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the distillation apparatus and separate it from the water.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities or debris.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. The oil's antimicrobial action also inhibits the growth of bacteria that cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes). Additionally, ginger oil's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that contribute to skin damage and inflammation, promoting healthy skin and reducing the severity of acne breakouts. This combination of actions makes ginger oil an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Germacrene: This sesquiterpene has antibacterial properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).
Curcumenol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the formation of acne lesions.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part for essential oil production due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne inflammation.
Roots: Roots are also used to produce essential oils that can help combat acne by reducing inflammation and fighting bacterial infections.

Buds: Ginger buds are used for essential oil production, which contains compounds that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help control acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh ginger root in the early morning when its moisture content is highest.
Steam distill the ginger root in a still for 2 hours to extract its essential oil.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the distillate using a separating funnel.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter to remove impurities and improve its clarity.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its quality.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oils helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil contains triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne. It also has astringent properties, which can help to dry out pimples and reduce their appearance. Additionally, Calendula's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate acne, making it a natural and effective treatment option for those looking to reduce acne breakouts and promote healthy skin.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: This flavonoid helps with acne by reducing inflammation and fighting off free radicals that can contribute to the development of acne.
Oleanolic acid: This triterpenoid has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
Neryl acetate: This sesquiterpene has antibacterial properties, which can help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used to make essential oils for acne due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: Used to make essential oils for acne due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
Stems: Used to make essential oils for acne due to their antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh calendula flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 35 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried flowers at 100 degrees celsius using a 1:5 flower to water ratio.
Collect the essential oil and separate it from the distillate within 24 hours of distillation.
Filter the essential oil through a coffee filter paper to remove any remaining impurities.
